title: rubus salteri calvatus
intro: Rubus salteri calvatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus salteri calvatus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Rubus salteri calvatus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in infusions and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
